step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the conclusion reached earlier. One additional point supporting the limitation of partial veneer crowns in short or tipped teeth is that these teeth may present increased risk of fracture due to reduced bulk and height compared to longer, straighter teeth. Given this higher susceptibility to damage, opting for a full-crown restoration provides greater protection and durability, reducing the likelihood of complications like chipping or breakage post-treatment. Furthermore, full-crowns distribute forces evenly around the entire circumference of the tooth, offering enhanced mechanical stability—a crucial factor especially if there's any concern about compromised structural integrity already existing in shorter or misaligned teeth.
